stemHtml: You cross a polar front jet stream in horizontal flight at approximately right angles. While crossing, in spite of a strong wind of 120 kt, you notice the temperature barely changes. Which of the following statements is correct?

options.a: This phenomenon does not surprise you at all, since normally no large temperature differences are possible at these heights.
options.b: This phenomenon is absolutely normal as you are crossing the jet core.
options.c: Since the result of such readings seems impossible, you will have the instruments tested after landing.
options.d: You assume the front associated with the jet stream to be very weak with practically no temperature difference between the two air masses.
